problem_statement: You are given a sorted array ГўВҖВҳarrГўВҖВҷ of length ГўВҖВҳnГўВҖВҷ, which contains positive integer positions of ГўВҖВҳnГўВҖВҷ gas stations on the X-axis. You are also given an integer ГўВҖВҳkГўВҖВҷ. You have to place 'k' new gas stations on the X-axis. You can place them anywhere on the non-negative side of the X-axis, even on non-integer positions. Let 'dist' be the maximum value of the distance between adjacent gas stations after adding k new gas stations. Find the minimum value of ГўВҖВҳdistГўВҖВҷ.

examples.explanation:
One of the possible ways to place 4 gas stations is {1,1.5,2,2.5,3,3.5,4,4.5,5}. Thus the maximum difference between adjacent gas stations is 0.5. Hence, the value of ГўВҖВҳdistГўВҖВҷ is 0.5. It can be shown that there is no possible way to add 4 gas stations in such a way that the value of ГўВҖВҳdistГўВҖВҷ is lower than this. 

Example 2:

One of the possible ways to place 1 gas station is {1,1.5,2,3,4,5,6,7,8,9,10}. Thus the maximum difference between adjacent gas stations is still 1. Hence, the value of ГўВҖВҳdistГўВҖВҷ is 1. It can be shown that there is no possible way to add 1 gas station in such a way that the value of ГўВҖВҳdistГўВҖВҷ is lower than this.
